description: auth0_flutter SDK added to pubspec.yaml dependencies
Has import for the auth0_flutter package (import package:auth0_flutter/auth0_flutter.dart)
Uses the Auth0 mobile class (not Auth0Web)
Uses webAuthentication() for Web Auth login/logout
Uses the CredentialsManager for token storage
Restores session via hasValidCredentials() / credentials() on startup
Adds Android manifestPlaceholders (auth0Domain, auth0Scheme) to build.gradle
Sets auth0Domain / auth0Scheme placeholder values for Android
Auth0 domain dev-example.auth0.com passed to the Auth0 constructor
Client ID abc123def456ghi789jkl012 passed to the Auth0 constructor
Does not use web-specific APIs (Auth0Web, auth0_flutter_web.dart, auth0-spa-js)
No client_secret in native code (PKCE does not require one)
Solution correctly integrates Auth0 into a Flutter mobile app with login, logout, and credential handling
question: Does the solution correctly integrate Auth0 into a Flutter mobile (iOS/Android) app with: (1) working login via auth0.webAuthentication().login(), (2) logout via webAuthentication().logout() (and/or clearCredentials()), (3) credential handling via credentialsManager (hasValidCredentials / credentials), (4) Android manifestPlaceholders (auth0Domain, auth0Scheme) configured in build.gradle, and (5) correct Auth0(domain, clientId) initialization?
examples:
PASS: Uses Auth0(domain, clientId), webAuthentication().login() for login, webAuthentication().logout() for logout, credentialsManager.hasValidCredentials() on startup, and build.gradle has manifestPlaceholders.
FAIL: Uses Auth0Web instead of the Auth0 mobile class.
FAIL: Missing manifestPlaceholders in build.gradle.
FAIL: Only implements login without logout or session restore.

description: Uses current SDK version (2.1.0) in pubspec.yaml dependency
Requests offline_access scope to enable refresh token renewal
Handles Auth0-specific mobile error types (WebAuthenticationException / CredentialsManagerException) instead of generic catch-all
Contains skill-specific advanced patterns beyond basic SDK usage (offline_access, error types, session restore, manifestPlaceholders, version)
question: Check ONLY for these skill-specific advanced patterns (NOT basic SDK usage like Auth0, webAuthentication, login, logout, credentials). Look for at least 2 of: (1) SDK version 2.1.0 in pubspec.yaml, (2) offline_access scope in the login call, (3) Auth0-specific mobile error handling (WebAuthenticationException or CredentialsManagerException with flags like isUserCancelledException / isNoCredentialsFound / isTokenRenewFailed — not just generic catch), (4) Android manifestPlaceholders (auth0Domain + auth0Scheme) in build.gradle, (5) session restoration on startup via hasValidCredentials() + credentials() with branching logic. These are advanced patterns taught by the skill that are NOT part of standard quickstart knowledge. Answer YES only if at least 2 of these advanced patterns are present, NO otherwise.
examples:
PASS: Login call includes offline_access scope AND build.gradle declares manifestPlaceholders with auth0Domain/auth0Scheme.
PASS: pubspec.yaml declares ^2.1.0 AND code catches CredentialsManagerException checking isNoCredentialsFound.
FAIL: Code only uses basic Auth0().webAuthentication().login() with no offline_access, no error handling, no manifestPlaceholders.
FAIL: Correct basic structure but only catches generic Exception, never the Auth0 exception types.
